evidence: Zero runtime dependencies: package.json contains only devDependencies (SvelteKit, Tailwind, TypeScript, Vite)
No third-party runtime imports anywhere in src/; all imports are from '$lib/', relative paths, or 'svelte'
HTTP calls use native fetch API rather than pulling in axios or another HTTP client library
No duplicate libraries serving the same purpose
DevDependencies are minimal and purpose-aligned: framework (SvelteKit/Svelte), styling (Tailwind), language (TypeScript), build (Vite)
dependency_health.issues_preventing_higher_score: Minor: devDependency version ranges use caret (^) which allows major version drift across environments, though this is standard npm convention and unlikely to cause real problems. The dependency set is otherwise exemplary for this project's scope.

strengths: Zero runtime dependencies; the entire production bundle is framework-only code with no third-party runtime libraries
Uses native fetch instead of pulling in an HTTP client library for GitHub API calls
DevDependency set is minimal and cohesive: exactly the framework stack (SvelteKit + Svelte + Vite), styling (Tailwind), and language (TypeScript)
No duplicate or overlapping libraries serving the same purpose
No heavy dependencies pulled in for light use; the project achieves its goals with framework primitives alone
dependency_health.issue_character: No dependency health issues were identified. The project has an exceptionally lean dependency footprint appropriate for a static portfolio site.
dependency_health.score_rationale: This codebase has zero runtime dependencies, uses only essential devDependencies for its framework stack, avoids pulling in third-party libraries where native APIs suffice, and has no overlap or redundancy. Against the global anchors, this is strong (90+) territory: a developer working here would find the dependency set cohesive, current, and purposeful with no material issues. The minor friction of caret ranges in version pinning is standard convention and does not meaningfully impact developer experience.
